title: Coat of arms of Slovenia

infobox.value: Azure, a mountain of three peaks Argent, base barry wavy Argent Azure, in center chief three mullets of 6 Or lower, bordure Gules.

is an [emblem](/wiki/Emblem "Emblem")[[1]](#cite_note-1) that consists of a red bordered blue shield on which there is a stylised white [Mount Triglav](/wiki/Triglav "Triglav"), under which there are two wavy lines representing the sea and the rivers of the country. Above Mount Triglav, there are three golden six-pointed stars representing the [Counts of Celje](/wiki/Counts_of_Celje "Counts of Celje").[[2]](#cite_note-2) It was designed in 1991 by [Marko Pogačnik](/wiki/Marko_Poga%C4%8Dnik "Marko Pogačnik") and adopted on 24 June 1991.

first_paragraph: The **coat of arms of Slovenia** is an [emblem](/wiki/Emblem "Emblem")[[1]](#cite_note-1) that consists of a red bordered blue shield on which there is a stylised white [Mount Triglav](/wiki/Triglav "Triglav"), under which there are two wavy lines representing the sea and the rivers of the country. Above Mount Triglav, there are three golden six-pointed stars representing the [Counts of Celje](/wiki/Counts_of_Celje "Counts of Celje").[[2]](#cite_note-2) It was designed in 1991 by [Marko Pogačnik](/wiki/Marko_Poga%C4%8Dnik "Marko Pogačnik") and adopted on 24 June 1991.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

| Coat of arms of Slovenia | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| [Armiger](/wiki/Armiger "Armiger") | [Republic of Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia") |
| Adopted | 1991 |
| [Shield](/wiki/Shield_(heraldry) "Shield (heraldry)") | Azure, a mountain of three peaks Argent, base barry wavy Argent Azure, in center chief three mullets of 6 Or lower, bordure Gules. |

The **coat of arms of Slovenia** is an [emblem](/wiki/Emblem "Emblem")[[1]](#cite_note-1) that consists of a red bordered blue shield on which there is a stylised white [Mount Triglav](/wiki/Triglav "Triglav"), under which there are two wavy lines representing the sea and the rivers of the country. Above Mount Triglav, there are three golden six-pointed stars representing the [Counts of Celje](/wiki/Counts_of_Celje "Counts of Celje").[[2]](#cite_note-2) It was designed in 1991 by [Marko Pogačnik](/wiki/Marko_Poga%C4%8Dnik "Marko Pogačnik") and adopted on 24 June 1991.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coat_of_arms_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Habsburg dynasty

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coat_of_arms_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Habsburg dynasty")]

Historically the modern-day territory of Slovenia consisted of a number of historical lands and territories which were eventually all ruled by the Austrian [House of Habsburg](/wiki/House_of_Habsburg "House of Habsburg").
Until the dissolution of [Austria-Hungary](/wiki/Austria-Hungary "Austria-Hungary"), the [Slovene Lands](/wiki/Slovene_Lands "Slovene Lands") did not have a coat of arms representing the whole nation; instead, (most of) the constituent lands had their own coats of arms:

* [Windic March](/wiki/Windic_March "Windic March"), later part of the [County of Cilli](/wiki/County_of_Cilli "County of Cilli")
* [County of Cilli](/wiki/County_of_Cilli "County of Cilli"), later part of the Duchy of Carniola
* [Duchy of Carniola](/wiki/Duchy_of_Carniola "Duchy of Carniola")
* southern [Duchy of Carinthia](/wiki/Duchy_of_Carinthia "Duchy of Carinthia")
* Lower [Duchy of Styria](/wiki/Lower_Styria "Lower Styria")
* [Slovene March](/wiki/Slovene_March_(Kingdom_of_Hungary) "Slovene March (Kingdom of Hungary)") in the [Vas county](/wiki/Vas_(former_county) "Vas (former county)") of the [Kingdom of Hungary](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Hungary "Kingdom of Hungary"), with the adjacent zones of the [Zala](/wiki/Zala_(former_county) "Zala (former county)") county ([Beltinci](/wiki/Beltinci "Beltinci"), [Turnišče](/wiki/Turni%C5%A1%C4%8De "Turnišče"), [Velika Polana](/wiki/Velika_Polana "Velika Polana"), [Kobilje](/wiki/Kobilje "Kobilje"))
* [County of Gorizia and Gradisca](/wiki/County_of_Gorizia_and_Gradisca "County of Gorizia and Gradisca"), later part of the [Austrian Littoral](/wiki/Austrian_Littoral "Austrian Littoral")
* [Istria](/wiki/Istria "Istria") (in the modern municipalities of [Koper](/wiki/Koper "Koper"), [Izola](/wiki/Izola "Izola"), [Piran](/wiki/Piran "Piran"), [Hrpelje-Kozina](/wiki/Hrpelje-Kozina "Hrpelje-Kozina"), [Muggia](/wiki/Muggia "Muggia") and [Dolina](/wiki/San_Dorligo_della_Valle "San Dorligo della Valle")), part of the [Austrian Littoral](/wiki/Austrian_Littoral "Austrian Littoral")

* [![Arms of the Windic March](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ea/Arms_of_Windic_March.svg/120px-Arms_of_Windic_March.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Arms_of_Windic_March.svg "Arms of the Windic March")

  Arms of the Windic March
* [![Combined arms of the Counts of Cilli and Sannock](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1d/Counts_of_Celje_coat_of_arms_%281-4%29.svg/120px-Counts_of_Celje_coat_of_arms_%281-4%29.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Counts_of_Celje_coat_of_arms_(1-4).svg "Combined arms of the Counts of Cilli and Sannock")

  Combined arms of the Counts of Cilli and Sannock

  Arms of the Duchy of Carniola
* [![Arms of the Duchy of Carinthia](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e4/Kaernten_shield_CoA.svg/120px-Kaernten_shield_CoA.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Kaernten_shield_CoA.svg "Arms of the Duchy of Carinthia")

  Arms of the Duchy of Carinthia
* [![Arms of the Duchy of Styria](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a5/Steiermark_Wappen_%28shield%29.svg/120px-Steiermark_Wappen_%28shield%29.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Steiermark_Wappen_(shield).svg "Arms of the Duchy of Styria")

  Arms of the Duchy of Styria

  Arms of the County of Gorizia and Gradisca

  Arms of the Austrian Littoral

### Kingdom of Yugoslavia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coat_of_arms_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Kingdom of Yugoslavia")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4f/Coat_of_arms_of_the_Kingdom_of_Yugoslavia_%28small%29.svg/250px-Coat_of_arms_of_the_Kingdom_of_Yugoslavia_%28small%29.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Coat_of_arms_of_the_Kingdom_of_Yugoslavia_(small).svg)

Lesser coat of arms of [Yugoslavia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Yugoslavia "Kingdom of Yugoslavia") from 1918 to 1941 with the Slovenian arms in the lower half

When the [State of Slovenes, Croats and Serbs](/wiki/State_of_Slovenes,_Croats_and_Serbs "State of Slovenes, Croats and Serbs") merged with [Kingdom of Serbia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Serbia "Kingdom of Serbia"), Slovenia had its [first real coat of arms](/wiki/Emblem_of_Yugoslavia "Emblem of Yugoslavia"), which actually was a heavily modified one from the Serbian Kingdom. The coat of arms presents the Serbian shield with white cross on the left, the Croatian shield on the right and under both there is a blue shield representing Slovenes. An image of the royal Yugoslav coat of arms can be seen on the 10-[Yugoslav dinar](/wiki/Yugoslav_dinar "Yugoslav dinar") banknote of 1926.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

### Socialist Republic of Slovenia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coat_of_arms_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Socialist Republic of Slovenia")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/da/Coat_of_Arms_of_the_Socialist_Republic_of_Slovenia.svg/250px-Coat_of_Arms_of_the_Socialist_Republic_of_Slovenia.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Coat_of_Arms_of_the_Socialist_Republic_of_Slovenia.svg)

The former emblem of the [Socialist Republic of Slovenia](/wiki/Socialist_Republic_of_Slovenia "Socialist Republic of Slovenia") (1945–1991)

The emblem of the Socialist Republic of Slovenia was designed by [Branko Simčič](/w/index.php?title=Branko_Sim%C4%8Di%C4%8D&action=edit&redlink=1 "Branko Simčič (page does not exist)") on the basis of the symbol of the [Liberation Front of the Slovene Nation](/wiki/Liberation_Front_of_the_Slovene_Nation "Liberation Front of the Slovene Nation"). It is in the style of [socialist emblem](/wiki/Socialist_heraldry "Socialist heraldry") of other communist nations. The sea and the mount [Triglav](/wiki/Triglav "Triglav") motive appeared in the emblem of the Socialist Republic of Slovenia, one of six constituent republics of the [Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia](/wiki/Socialist_Federal_Republic_of_Yugoslavia "Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia"). The former emblem was rounded by [wheat](/wiki/Wheat "Wheat") with [linden](/wiki/Tilia "Tilia") leaves and featured a [red star](/wiki/Red_star "Red star") at the top. There was also the other emblem which presented Yugoslavia as a whole.

### Republic of Slovenia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coat_of_arms_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Republic of Slovenia")]

A tender for the design of a new coat of arms of Slovenia was published in May 1991. It was won by Marko Pogačnik, whose design was meant to represent Slovenia as a macroregional entity, and to follow in the footsteps of the poet [France Prešeren](/wiki/France_Pre%C5%A1eren "France Prešeren") and the architect [Jože Plečnik](/wiki/Jo%C5%BEe_Ple%C4%8Dnik "Jože Plečnik"): the arms reflect the description of nature in Prešeren's 19th-century [epic](/wiki/Epic_poetry "Epic poetry") *[The Baptism on the Savica](/wiki/The_Baptism_on_the_Savica "The Baptism on the Savica")*, and are based on Plečnik's informal 1934 proposal for a new coat of arms of Slovenia (in the from of a pillar dedicated to the Virgin Mary outside the [parish church of Bled](/wiki/St._Martin%27s_Parish_Church,_Bled "St. Martin's Parish Church, Bled").)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Pogačnik's design was proclaimed the new official coat of arms of Slovenia with the [constitutional amendment](/wiki/Constitution_of_Slovenia "Constitution of Slovenia") C 100, which took effect on 24 June 1991.

### Historical symbols

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coat_of_arms_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Historical symbols")]

  Coat of arms of the [Duchy of Carniola](/wiki/Duchy_of_Carniola "Duchy of Carniola") (until 1918)

* [![Emblem of German-collaborationist Slovene Home Guard (1943–1945)](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f3/Znak_Slovenskega_Domobranstva.svg/120px-Znak_Slovenskega_Domobranstva.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Znak_Slovenskega_Domobranstva.svg "Emblem of German-collaborationist Slovene Home Guard (1943–1945)")

## Symbolics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coat_of_arms_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Symbolics")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/74/Grb_Republike_Slovenije_-_geometrijsko_pravilo.GIF/250px-Grb_Republike_Slovenije_-_geometrijsko_pravilo.GIF)](/wiki/File:Grb_Republike_Slovenije_-_geometrijsko_pravilo.GIF)

The geometrical rule for the construction of the national coat of arms of Slovenia

The designer, [Marko Pogačnik](/wiki/Marko_Poga%C4%8Dnik "Marko Pogačnik"), has described the coat of arms as a [cosmogram](/wiki/Cosmogram "Cosmogram"), whose purpose is to create an "energetic field" intended to protect the country and stimulate its inner potential.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

According to Pogačnik, the silhouette of mount Triglav symbolises the male principle, while the two wavy lines below it - representing the [Triglav Lakes Valley](/wiki/Triglav_Lakes_Valley "Triglav Lakes Valley"), the Slovene seacoast, and rivers in general - symbolise the female principle. The three golden mullets above Triglav, taken from the coat of arms of the [Counts of Celje](/wiki/Counts_of_Celje "Counts of Celje"),[[6]](#cite_note-6) symbolize [democracy](/wiki/Democracy "Democracy").

## Legal status

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coat_of_arms_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Legal status")]

As a work of arts, published in the official journal *[Official Gazette](/w/index.php?title=Official_Gazette_(Slovenia)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Official Gazette (Slovenia) (page does not exist)")*, the national coat of arms of Slovenia qualifies as an official work and is per Article 9 of the Slovene Copyright and Related Rights Act not protected by the copyrights.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Its usage is regulated by the *Act Regulating the Coat-of-Arms, Flag and Anthem of the Republic of Slovenia and the Flag of the Slovene Nation*, published in the Official Gazette in 1994.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Criticism

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coat_of_arms_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Criticism")]

The coat of arms has been criticized by Aleksander Hribovšek of the Heraldica Slovenica society as heraldically deficient: Mount Triglav (whose choice as a national symbol he questions) and the sea are not represented according to heraldic rules, and the overall arms have no official [blazon](/wiki/Blazon "Blazon"); the form of shield used is also not heraldically recognised.[[9]](#cite_note-9)
